iit jodhpur redirecttologinpage director professor avinash kumar agarwal director iit jodhpur professor avinash kumar agarwal took over the charge of director iit jodhpur on may he obtained his undergraduate degree in mechanical engineering from malviya regional engineering college jaipur and his mtech energy and phd energy from the indian institute of technology iit delhi after his post doctoral fellowship at the erc uw madison usa he returned to india in and joined iit kanpur he was visiting professor at the university of loughborough uk photonics institute university of vienna austria hanyang university and kaist south korea prof agarwal is interested in research in ic engines combustion conventional and alternative fuels methanol dme hydrogen hcng fuelled engine development lca and tco analyses fuel sprays lubricating oil tribology optical diagnostics laser ignition hcci particulates and emission control and large bore engines he has developed laser fired hydrogen and cng engines in automotive sizes and developed the first electronic fuel injection system equipped with locomotive engine for indian railways currently prof agarwal is developing methanol and dme fuelled engines vehicles for the automotive agricultural sectors prof agarwal has published over peer reviewed international journal and conference papers edited books and book chapters attracting scopus and google scholar citations prof agarwal is the editor of fuel editor in chief of the journal of energy and environmental sustainability associate editor of the asme journal of energy resources technology asme open journal of engineering international journal of vehicle systems modelling and testing and the journal of the institute of engineers series and an editorial board member of imeche international journal of engine research he edited wiley vch germany handbook of combustion volumes pages the most updated combustion compilation globally for his outstanding contributions prof agarwal is conferred upon sir bose national fellowship by serb sae india foundation guru award wsset innovation award clarivate analytics india citation award in engineering and technology prestigious shanti swarup bhatnagar prize in engineering sciences rajib goyal prize in physical sciences nasi reliance industries platinum jubilee award inae silver jubilee young engineer award dr raman young teachers award sae ralph teetor educational award insa young scientist award uict young scientist award inae young engineer award devendra shukla research fellowship poonam and prabhu goyal endowed chair professorship sbi endowed chair professorship at iit kanpur aicte career award for young teachers dst young scientist award and dst boyscast fellowship in addition to inaugural version of distinguished alumni award by mnit jaipur and distinguished alumni award by iit delhi prof agarwal is highly cited researcher in and is among the top ten hcrs from india among hcr researchers globally in fields of enquiry he is india number one energy researcher in the recently declared stanford university listing of the top of researchers globally he is an elected fellow of the society of automotive engineers international usa sae american society of mechanical engineers asme indian national academy of engineering inae international society for energy environment and sustainability isees royal society of chemistry rsc national academy of science allahabad nasi world society of sustainable energy technologies wsset american association for advancement in science aaas and combustion institute usa ci he is featured in the dst golden jubilee coffee table book under scientists shaping today india released by vigyan prasar government of india on national science day february th at iit kanpur prof agarwal has established state of the art engine research laboratory www iitk ac in erl and he was also the founder director of iit kanpur science and technology research park technopark iitk he also founded the international society for energy environment and sustainability www isees in in and is the editor of the book series entitled energy environment and sustainability published by springer singapore onwards important links cccd iitj recruitment correspondence rti tenders techscape contact old website nccr portal how to reach iitj institute repository redirecttologinpage all research highlights language independent speech generation system various preordain situations result in disease or injury to people and deprive them off with their natural ability to communicate verbally the diseases which affect the voice and language of people may include congenital impairments such as cerebral palsy intellectual impairment and autism and acquired conditions such as amyotrophic lateral sclerosis and parkinson disease as larynx is the main organ for voice production any illness which results in the removal of larynx laryngectomy will result in loss of voice the illness may be malignancy of larynx or pharynx or severe laryngeal stenosis the afflicted people find other means like alaryngeal methods which include hand written notes by simple actions like hand or facial gestures with esophageal speech or with aid of devices like electrolarynx or tracheo esophageal prosthesis the means of sign language require the use of hand gestures and or facial expressions sign language is mode of communication for patients who are affected since birth in recent years the technological advancement in the field of electro medical devices for life support implantable biomedical devices and wearable medical devices have successfully provided artificial abilities to the afflicted people related to any kind of disability or impairment the electro medical devices implantable biomedical devices and wearable medical devices may include but are not limited to an artificial pacemaker ventricular assist device bioartificial liver wearable dialysis device implantable artificial kidney devices artificial limbs and sensory devices such as hearing aids optical lenses etc the electro medical devices when used in combination with multiple speech synthesis techniques provide an option for the people with speech and language impairment to communicate audibly such electro medical devices are known as augmentative and alternative communication aac devices or speech generation devices sgds in the speech synthesis techniques the spoken language is generated by machine on the basis of written input the present subject matter describes methods and systems for automatically generating speech which will be language independent and facilitate the communication between mute people and normal people in an example implementation of the present subject matter electrical signals are generated by first set of sensors wearable on combination of thumb finger and or wrist of first hand of user to produce an electrical signal depending on bending of at least one of the thumb the finger and or the wrist of the first hand the electrical signals are also generated by second set of sensors wearable on combination of thumb finger and or wrist of second hand of user to produce an electrical signal depending on bending of at least one of the thumb the finger and or the wrist of the second hand the magnitude of the received electrical signals is compared with plurality of pre defined combination of magnitudes stored in memory by using the signal processing unit pre defined combination of magnitude of the plurality of pre defined combination of magnitudes is associated to phonetic corresponding to at least one of consonant and vowel in an example implementation the consonant and the vowel can be from hindi language phonetics phonetic is assigned to the received electrical signals based on the comparison an audio signal is generated by an audio transmitter corresponding to the assigned phonetic and based on trained data associated with vocal characteristics stored in machine learning unit the generation of the audio signals according to the phonetics having combination of vowels and consonants leads to the generation of speech and enables the mute people to audibly communicate with normal people the speech synthesis technique of the present subject matter uses phonetics and therefore the speech generation is independent of any language reference india patent application publication date author of the article dr sumit kalra dr arpit khandelwal in collaboration with aiims jodhpur important links cccd iitj recruitment correspondence rti tenders techscape contact old website nccr portal how to reach iitj institute repository donations web policy web information manager feedback cert in help nirf internal committee redirecttologinpage all research highlights research paper published by dr sushmita jha in scientific reports identification of markers for deadly brain tumor multi institute team led by dr sushmita jha department of bioscience and bioengineering iit jodhpur has identified new markers for deadly brain tumour glioblastoma in the paper published in the international journal scientific reports nature publishing group dr jha team used multipronged approach utilizing data from the cancer genome atlas experimental data generated from normal cells and brain tumour cells cultured in the laboratory and experimental data generated from human brain tissue to identify novel biomarkers for glioma team members include dr sushmita paul assistant professor dept of bioscience and bioengineering iitj dr epari sridhar department of pathology tata memorial hospital mumbai maharashtra india phd students dr nidhi sharma ms shalini singh ms shivanjali saxena mr ishan agrawal and tech students ms varsha srinivasan ms swetha mr arvind this study was funded by the department of science and technology dst and department of biotechnology dbt government of india this is the first report identifying nlrp as candidate prognostic marker for glioma progression leading the way for designing future therapeutic strategies background gliomas are the most prevalent intracranial tumours accounting for of primary malignant brain tumors based on the degree of malignancy gliomas are classified into low and high grade low grade glioma lgg specifically represents of all central nervous system tumors in children high grade gliomas glioblastomas account for of cns tumors in india even after surgery chemotherapy and radiotherapy the median survival is less than months gliomas are tumors arising from glial cells glial cells constitute approximately of the human brain they are responsible for maintenance immunity and repair the recalcitrance of glioblastomas gbm results from their ability to form new blood vessels via angiogenesis and tumor heterogeneity the highly infiltrative behavior of gbm makes surgery nearly ineffective since tumor cells and glioblastoma stem cells colonize the surrounding brain tissue causing relapses even at distant brain sites the nucleotide binding domain and leucine rich repeat containing receptors nlrs and absent in melanoma aim are innate immune receptors crucial for initiation and progression of several cancers there is dearth of reports linking nlrs and aim to glioma pathology we used data driven approach to identify nlrs aim and nlr associated gene expression and methylation patterns in low grade glioma and glioblastoma using the cancer genome atlas tcga patient datasets since tcga data is obtained from tumor tissue comprising of multiple cell populations including glioma cells endothelial cells and tumor associated microglia macrophages we carried out cell culture experiments and immunohistochemistry on human brain tissue to identify cell specific effects tcga data mining showed significant differential nlr regulation and strong correlation with survival in different grades of glioma we report differential expression and methylation of nlrs in glioma followed by nlrp identification as candidate prognostic marker for glioma progression in the normal brain cells known as microglia function as immune cells we found that nlrp deficient microglia show increased colony formation while nlrp deficient glioma cells show decreased cellular proliferation immunohistochemistry of human glioma tissue shows increased nlrp expression interestingly microglia show reduced migration towards nlrp deficient glioma cells the most significant part of the work is identification of the protein nlrp that is involved in the normal immune response when absent in microglia leads to aberrant cellular proliferation moreover this protein is shown to be highly expressed in tumour cells future studies from our group are aimed at understanding the molecular signaling pathways this may allow for development of targeted therapeutics and increased survival important links cccd iitj recruitment correspondence rti tenders techscape archive sr no description model development to predict shrinkage defects during solidification of pure metal important links cccd iitj recruitment correspondence rti tenders techscape contact old downward uxg accessibility tool accessibility options ctrl bigger text smaller text text spacing line height dyslexia friendly adhd mode saturation low saturation high saturation desaturate light dark invert colors highlight links text to speech cursor pause animation hide images reset all settings created by ctrl accessibility options open the accessibility option rate this translation do you like to give feedback submit all research highlights indian institute of technology jodhpur iit jodhpur redirecttologinpage all research highlights model development to predict shrinkage defects during solidification of pure metal the phase change phenomenon associated with solidification process is extensively confronted in numerous practical operations such as production processes involving welding and metal casting thermal sprays latent heat thermal storage thermal management for electronic cooling thermal comfort etc one of the most significant quests of the relevance for solidification processes is to discern how substantial are the phenomenon of the casting defects existing commercial solvers to predict for metal alloy solidification processes commonly produce outcomes associated with transient thermal field and these outcomes are not analogous to the understanding of the position of the shrinkage cavities within the cast products therefore it is essential to consider the behaviour of the melt convection during solidification process in order to predict the shrinkage induced defects during solidification which is yet to be fully explored in the technical framework shrinkage is explained as variation in the size of the cast product owing to the density difference between liquid and solid phases in particular three types of shrinkages are accountable for shrinkage defects liquid shrinkage solidification shrinkage and solid shrinkage leading to the development of cracks shrinkage cavities or buckling of the cast significant amount of solidification shrinkage is witnessed owing to the density variation between solid and liquid phases during the solidification process and solidification shrinkage attributes to the most severe deformation of the final cast product numerical prediction of solidification shrinkage arising at the time of producing the solidified cast product from the molten metal is of primary focus of the present study considerable difference between the solid and liquid phase densities causes an overall volume change of the final cast product with undesirable shrinkage defects like macro or micro open shrinkage and macro or micro porosity such defects cause large number of casting rejections leading to an overall increase in production cost supervising the cast defects like porosity shrinkage cavity and macro segregation is significant element that contributes towards assuring the quality of cast metals however numerical analysis of shrinkage induced defects poses formidable challenges associated with complex interactions between distinct phases like solid liquid and void till date majority of the studies involving shrinkage defect are experimental in nature leading to widely varying empirical understanding of the phenomena therefore development of physics based numerical modelling is of absolute importance the present study focuses on developing novel numerical model by integrating enthalpy updating method with volume of fluid vof method to track the evolution of solid front growth during the solidification of pure metal leading to the prediction of the shrinkage defect in the final cast product the flow field generated within the melt during the solidification process due to combined buoyancy and shrinkage effect plays major role in defining the shape of the shrinkage defect in the final cast product the proposed model is competent to capture shrinkage induced convection and free surface evolution without applying any superficial numerical treatment this model will be of significant relevance to the solidification community across academia and industry for predicting shrinkage defects in final cast products the study also opens up possibilities of farther adaptation of the model for predicting shrinkage defects during alloy solidification and shrinkage defects encountered for complex casting geometry important links cccd iitj recruitment technology jodhpur iit jodhpur redirecttologinpage all research highlights optical field design for solar thermal applications the present investigation focusses on optical layout for the design of medium to large scale kw mw solar field based on concentrated solar power csp technology using heliostats with central receiver system crs the implementation of such solar field can be carried out where direct normal incidence dni is moderate to high thus reducing the dependence on fossil fuel background among the various renewable energies solar thermal energy ste has attracted attention of researcher due to its direct applicability in various industries in particular concentrated solar power csp is widely implemented for this purpose as high temperature is reached due to high optical concentration which in turn leads to higher efficiencies and thus making it as preferred system in this direction heliostat field with central tower provide an ideal candidate and thus designing heliostat field layout is an essential component of it heliostat field comprises of large size mirrors that reflects the incident solar radiation onto receiver as the sun moves during the day as well as throughout the year the challenging task is estimating the number of heliostats required to produce the desired thermal output the parameters that affects heliostat field depend on geographical location latitude and longitude shadow associated with given heliostat dimension cosine losses spacing between the two adjacent heliostats blocking article written by narayanan image credits sanjoy chatterjee and pedamallu vvnsp raju link to the present work erjee paper pdf research domain lasers and optics important links cccd iitj recruitment correspondence rti tenders techscape contact old rate this translation do you like to give feedback submit all research highlights indian institute of technology jodhpur iit jodhpur redirecttologinpage all research highlights novel multipath mitigation technique for gnss signals in urban scenarios global navigation satellite system gnss based positioning technologies are poised to play crucial role in providing location based services that are used for strategic purposes emergency safety of life services and for commercial purposes in urban and suburban settings presence of multiple reflectors and scatterers like high rise buildings towers vehicles trees cause the transmitted signal to reach gnss receiver through multiple paths gnss receiver while tracking the navigation signal can potentially get locked to any of the signal replicas coming from the transmitter leading to range error in the navigation solution therefore multipath propagation poses serious threat to the accuracy of position solutions determined by gnss receivers in recent study professor arun kumar singh and his group from the indian institute of technology iit jodhpur has proposed novel multipath mitigation technique that provides significant improvements in range accuracies over the current state of the art techniques like narrow correlator nc and high resolution correlator hrc or double delta correlator the performance improvements are valid for general multipath models encountered in the land mobile satellite channels the performance guarantees include closed form expressions for the probability of correct delay estimate and simulation results for the average range error variation with respect to carrier to noise ratio cnr signal bandwidth and different channel settings the range error caused due to multipath propagation can be dealt with in two possible ways multipath estimation or multipath mitigation in general multipath estimation techniques involve optimization of the conditional probability function over range of multipath delays amplitudes and phases their computational complexity is very high and they are not suitable for implementation on smart devices that operate with limited power the current state of the art multipath mitigation techniques include nc and hrc nc and hrc perform reasonably well in the scenarios where the number of multipaths is small and line of sight los component has highest amplitude amongst all multipaths in most pertinent urban scenarios there can be large number of multipath and los may not always have the highest power among all the paths therefore novel multipath mitigation solutions were needed to overcome limitations of current state of the art techniques the present study to be published in ieee transactions on vehicular technology provides novel multipath mitigation technique that is able to estimate ranges with very high probability even in the presence of multipaths the proposed technique provides improved accuracy as compared to the currently known best multipath mitigation techniques the performance guarantees hold for the cnr of as low as db for the typical vehicle speeds kmph as well as for high speeds kmph and for different multipath scenarios including urban sub urban metropolitan environments in the proposed technique double differentiated correlation function based histogram output is used to estimate the range the statistical independence of channel parameters which change after every coherence time is exploited in order to improve the probability of correct estimate the results are validated using both statistical models from ieee and measurement based land mobile satellite channel models developed through field trials by dlr germany and also standardized by international telecom union itu the new technique will be pave way for use of gnss receivers in urban scenarios for future generation smart vehicles and high accuracy based location services important links cccd iitj recruitment correspondence rti tenders open the accessibility option rate this translation do you like to give feedback submit all research highlights indian institute of technology jodhpur iit jodhpur redirecttologinpage all research highlights rajasthani clay catalytic converters for automobiles the current discovery indicate new non rare earth non noble metal based catalytic convertor using rajasthani clay the catalytic convertor is developed by cleaver use iron nickel cobalt nanoparticles in rajasthani clay the metal oxides work as oxygen reservoir and could completely oxidize nox cox and hydrocarbons at lower than oc temperature background automobile industries are facing more and stricter emissions regulations in an effort to reduce the amount of harmful air pollutants that are released into the environment related to nox cox and soot along with particulate matter currently one method of reducing harmful emissions is three way catalytic twc converter that reduces harmful nox to and oxidizes co to co and oxidizes residual hydrocarbons to co and water however it requires the use of the rare earth element cerium ce and palladium which are increasing in price and suffering from supply problems lately these expensive metals make the the catalyst convertor one of the costliest part of car with price from each catalytic convertor have about gm of pure palladium which cost around per gram more expensive than gold despite that cost the performance of catalytic convertors go down with time mostly in years while approved life of car is about years the research team of prof sharma has been working on find an alternate solution by understanding the catalytic convertor deactivation and looking for cheaper solution for the same his team has found that with time palladium particle spread over ceria undergo surface oxidation and decompose in small particle due to high temperature oxidation process degree so there were three challenges reduction of temperature replacement of palladium and cerium with non nobel metal and re design the convertor to prevent metal deactivation the fe ni co cooperative nano particles were used in the isolated nanospheres work as single site catalyst where hafniarajasthani clay used as oxygen reservoir the catalytic performance was found to be better than conventional convertor at degree article written by rakesh sharma image credits rakesh sharma redirecttologinpage all research highlights structure and exfoliation mechanism of two dimensional boron nanosheets tittle of the paper structure and exfoliation mechanism of two dimensional boron nanosheets nature communications dr pranay ranjan department of metallurgical and materials engineering iit jodhpur description exfoliation of two dimensional nanosheets from three dimensional non layered non van der waals crystals represents an emerging strategy for materials engineering that could significantly increase the library of materials yet the exfoliation mechanism in which nanosheets are derived from crystals that are not intrinsically layered remains unclear here we show that planar defects in the starting boron material promote the exfoliation of boron sheets by combining liquid phase exfoliation aberration corrected scanning transmission electron microscopy raman spectroscopy and density functional theory calculations we demonstrate that boron nanosheets consist of planar arrangement of icosahedral sub units cleaved along the planes of rhombohedral boron correspondingly intrinsic stacking faults in boron form parallel layers of faulted planes in the same orientation as the exfoliated nanosheets reducing the cleavage energy planar defects represent potential engineerable pathway for exfoliating sheets from boron and more broadly the other covalently bonded materials figure liquid phase exfoliation of boron schematic depiction of products through liquid phase exfoliation from van der waals vdw crystals isotropic polycrystalline materials and boron scanning electron microscopy sem images of the starting boron material sample sigma aldrich sample alfa aesar and sample yamanaka advanced materials converged beam transmission electron microscopy tem image and corresponding high angle annular darkfield haadf scanning transmission electron microscopy stem image of boron sheets suspended on holey carbon tem grid figure role of planar defects in the exfoliation mechanism of boron tem bf image of boron sample with high density of planar defects in the bottom right crystal grain bf stem image of rhombohedral boron projected along the zone axis showing sequence of parallel planar defects including twins and stacking faults dashed lines indicate the defect plane the icosahedral columns are represented as red ringed circles for easier visualization of the symmetry breaking due to the defects the magnified images of the twin boundary and stacking fault sf boxed in light blue and red respectively are shown on the right with the faulted plane marked by the black arrows model of the unit cell projected in the depicting the atoms red circles forming the defect planes density functional theory dft calculated energies for the formation of the and ii surfaces from the pristine crystal and from the planar defects schematic of the defect mediated exfoliation process viewed from the orientation the icosahedral columns are represented as red ringed circles stacking faults as dashed lines and symmetry breaking of the crystal as solid lines the black arrows represent the cleaving of the crystal from the stacking faults article by dr pranay ranjan link research domain materials borophene important links cccd iitj recruitment correspondence rti tenders techscape contact old website nccr portal how to reach iitj do you like to give feedback submit eligibility criteria and selection process school of management entrepreneurship iit jodhpur redirecttologinpage eligibility criteria and selection process the admission into the flagship master of business administration mba program of the school of management and entrepreneurship sme leading to the mba or the mba technology degree is based on the criteria and process as indicated below note during the first year students complete foundational courses across various disciplines in the second year they can select one of two tracks leading to either an mba or an mba technology note students completing their first year at sme iit jodhpur also have an opportunity to pursue their second year at one of the partner schools abroad suny albany business school usa brandeis international business school usa george washington university usa and case western reserve university usa resulting in the international dualdegree eligibility criterion qualifying degree minimum of bachelor degree or an equivalent qualification awarded by any university established by an act of the central or state legislature in india or by other educational institutions established by an act of parliament or recognised as deemed university under section of the ugc act additionally the bachelor degree or equivalent must entail minimum of three years of education following the completion of higher secondary schooling or its equivalent note final year students of the qualifying degree are also eligible to apply such candidates if selected will be admitted provisionally on the condition that they meet all requirements of the qualifying degree the candidate must submit all certificates related to the qualifying degree up to the current completed semesters years additionally the candidate must provide provisional certificate of completion of the qualifying degree within two months of joining the program if selected failure to meet these requirements will lead to the cancellation of the candidature for the program minimum percentage of marks in the qualifying degree minimum of marks in aggregate of all the years semesters which should be equivalent to first class in the university conversion rule this is applicable for general obc ews categories or ii cgpa cumulative grade point average on point scale which should be equivalent to first class in the university conversion rule this is applicable for general obc ews categories note the admission eligibility requirements may be relaxed for qualifying degree by on point for sc st and pwd candidates the admission eligibility requirements may be relaxed for qualifying degree by on point scale on point ii for sc st and pwd candidates the above relaxation shall be considered only as qualifying criteria valid score in the common admission test cat conducted by the indian institutes of management admission process the admission process for the mba program will consist of two steps initial shortlisting and final shortlisting the criteria for each step are outlined below initial shortlisting an initial shortlist will be created based on the composite score calculated using the following parameters and corresponding weights relaxations for all reserved categories will be applied in accordance with the norms of the ministry of education government of india amended from time to time cat overall scaled score work experience full time gender diversity note cat cutoffs may be applied to shortlist candidates and these cutoffs could be based on the overall cat percentile or sectional cat percentiles or both the cutoff percentiles will be considered based on the number of applications received note work experience gained in coaching centres self proprietary small scale businesses like retail stores kirana stores ngos or petrol pumps gas stations will not be considered at all work experience of or more days in month will be counted as month while experience of fewer than days will not be counted experience letters and the first and last month salary slips must be provided at the time of the interview any claims of work experience not supported by pay slips or formal relieving letter from the respective organisation as specified in the mba program application will be deemed invalid and will result in disqualification from the entire selection process the cutoff date for calculating work experience is the day the application for mba admissions goes live no consideration will be given to any time spent as an intern either during or after the course of study or any periods of apprenticeship such as articleship required for certain professional courses or any unpaid work undertaken in any organisation or institution the final discretion regarding the type level and extent of work experience lies with the institute authorities final shortlisting candidates from all categories shortlisted based on the composite score will be called for personal interview via virtual mode ii the final shortlist will be prepared based on the following criteria with due weights assigned to each component relaxations for all reserved categories will be applied in accordance with the norms of the ministry of education government of india amended from time to time composite score personal interview for international applicants eligibility as stated earlier in point and of eligibility criterion the legality and equivalence of the degree can be verified with the help of the association of indian universities aiu admission process merit based based on graduate record examination gre score of and above or graduate management admission test gmat score of and above achieved within the last five years from the date of admission at sme iit jodhpur candidates joining while retaining employment with their current employer any candidate joining the mba program at sme iit jodhpur who is on study leave sabbatical or higher study leave must submit no objection certificate noc from their employer the noc should either state that the employer has no objection to the candidate applying for summer internship or final placement with another organisation or confirm that the candidate is required to undertake the summer internship exclusively with the employer and return to the same organisation after completing the program important dates online submission of the application form begins on th january online submission of the application form closes on th february extended th march interview schedule tentatively starts from th march note the above dates are only tentative and are liable to change iit jodhpur reserves the right to change modify add or remove any shortlisting and selection criteria as well as any other information in the mba program admissions document if deemed necessary at any time any disputes related to mba admissions will be subject to the jurisdiction of the competent courts within the territorial jurisdiction of jodhpur city only in case of any ambiguity or dispute regarding the interpretation the decision of the iit jodhpur shall be final and binding important links cccd iitj recruitment structure student intake for indian students program fee structure the tuition fee for the program is inr per semester in addition to the tuition fee students are required to pay the semester fee admission fee one time convocation fee one time refundable deposits one time dining fee and any other applicable charges students opting for the international dual degree program in their second year are required to pay the day scholar fees at iit jodhpur as well as any other applicable fees at the partner institute note students from sc st and pwd categories are exempt from paying the tuition fee as per government norms amended from time to time however they are still required to pay all other charges mentioned above the components of the semester fee are subject to change from time to time student intake the intake of the program is note norms of the ministry of education government of india shall be adhered to for the category wise breakup for international students program fee structure the tuition fee for international students from saarc countries will be times the fee applicable to indian students the tuition fee for international students from non saarc countries will be times the fee applicable to indian students all other components of the fee will remain the same as those applicable to indian students for all international students the components of the semester fee are subject to change from time to time student intake the intake of the program is important links cccd iitj recruitment correspondence rti tenders techscape contact old website nccr portal how process for mba admissions begin ans the application process will commence online from the day following the declaration of the cat results please refer to the link what is the minimum qualification required to apply for the mba program ans please refer to the link how should apply to the mba program online or by regular mail ans applications must be submitted online only please refer to the link how will my application for the mba program be evaluated ans please refer to the link is my cat score the only or the most important criterion for my candidature for the mba program ans while the cat scores are an important criterion for shortlisting it is not the sole determinant of candidature other factors such as performance in the personal interview full time work experience and gender diversity are also considered and carry weightage what is the application fee for the mba program ans please refer to the link is work experience mandatory for the mba program ans no work experience is not mandatory however full time work experience carries weightage in the selection process is the admission process different for the mba and mba technology degrees ans there is no separate admission process for the two degree streams once admitted to the program students may choose either the mba or mba technology degree after completing the first year provided they fulfil the prescribed program requirements what constitutes valid work experience ans please refer to the link are hostel facilities available what is the occupancy of the hostel rooms ans yes hostel facilities are available all mba students are required to reside on campus suitable accommodation is provided to each student subject to availability what is the course structure of the mba and mba technology programs ans please refer to the link what is the international dual degree program ans please refer to the link are the programs offered by foreign partner institutions under the international dual degree program conducted on campus ans yes please refer to the link are there additional expenses to be borne by student opting for the international dual degree program ans students opting for the international dual degree program in the second year are required to pay the day scholar fees at iit jodhpur along with any applicable fees at the partner institution the components of the semester fee are subject to change from time to time will student opting for the international dual degree be eligible for international placements will the foreign partner institution assist with placements ans yes students opting for the international dual degree are eligible to participate in the placement process of the foreign partner institution subject to the rules and regulations of the partner institution and the laws of the respective country iit jodhpur does not impose any restrictions in this regard is student opting for the international dual degree program eligible to participate in campus placements at iit jodhpur ans yes subject to the rules and conditions specified by the recruiter is there any upper limit on the number of students going abroad for the international dual degree program in year ans there is no fixed upper limit however the number may vary from year to year and students are selected primarily based on academic performance at sme and their past academic record as part of the international dual degree program can student complete the specialised master program at iit jodhpur instead of going abroad ans no the specialised master programs offered by partner institutions are regular on campus degree programs and require students to reside in the country where the partner institution is located can add or update information after submitting my application ans no once the application has been submitted no changes can be made are international students eligible to apply for the mba program ans yes please refer to the link important links cccd iit jodhpur redirecttologinpage all news prof ram prakash from the department of physics iit jodhpur has been elected as the president of the plasma science society of india during the th pssi national plasma symposium on plasma science and technology plasma held at iit tirupati between december department of physics iit jodhpur is delighted to share that professor ram prakash has been elected as the president of plasma science society of india pssi for the term of established in the plasma science society of india is premier national society dedicated to the promotion of plasma science and technology the society plays vital role in advancing research education and applications of plasma science across the country pssi actively fosters collaboration among researchers academic institutions national laboratories and industry through publications conferences educational initiatives and coordinated research programs it also works to popularize plasma science by supporting academic curricula public outreach and the development of plasma scholars for more details click here important links cccd iitj recruitment correspondence rti tenders techscape contact old website rate this translation do you like to give feedback submit research highlights physics iit jodhpur redirecttologinpage research highlights prof ram prakash from the department of physics iit jodhpur has been elected as the president of the plasma science society of india during the th pssi national plasma symposium on plasma science and technology plasma held at iit tirupati between december department of physics iit jodhpur is delighted to share that professor ram prakash has been elected as the president of plasma science society of india pssi for the term of established in the plasma science society of india is premier national society dedicated to the promotion of plasma science and technology the society plays vital role in advancing research education and applications of plasma science across the country pssi actively fosters collaboration among researchers academic institutions national laboratories and industry through publications conferences educational initiatives and coordinated research programs it also works to popularize plasma science by supporting academic curricula public outreach and the development of plasma scholars pssi has strong and diverse membership base comprising scientists and scholars from leading indian organizations including department of atomic energy dae institutes such as ipr gandhinagar barc mumbai rrcat indore sinp kolkata etc drdo laboratories such as mtrdc bengaluru etc department of space centers including prl ahmedabad sac ahmedabad etc csir laboratories such as csir immt bhubaneswar csir ceeri pilani npl new delhi etc and dst institutions like iasst guwahati etc faculty members and students from iisc bengaluru almost all iits selected nits universities and colleges across india along with industries engaged in plasma technologies are active members of the society plasma science is field where curiosity meets creativity and collaboration illuminates the path of discovery professor ram prakash presidentship is expected to further strengthen pssi mission and enhance its impact on the national and international plasma science community important links cccd iitj recruitment correspondence rti tenders techscape news research seminar by dr puneet sharma on dec at pm title of the talk dynamics of triangular systems journey through non autonomous route date time venue dec at pm at pm on seminar room dept of mathematics abstract in this talk we discuss the dynamics of general non autonomous dynamical systems we relate the dynamics of the system with the dynamics of generating maps we discuss properties such as various notions of transitivity and sensitivities equicontinuity minimality proximality and li yorke cells for such systems consequently we investigate dynamics of triangular systems and relate it with the dynamics of the generating non autonomous systems important links cccd iitj recruitment correspondence rti tenders techscape contact old website nccr portal how to reach iitj mechanical engineering indian institute of technology jodhpur and pi of fern lab has been honoured with the women in sensors wiley best paper award at the ieee fleps conference held in national university of singapore nus singapore the award recognizes her outstanding contribution to the field of flexible sensors through the paper titled assistive living and wireless emergency alert system for mobility impaired users this innovative work presents wearable wireless sensing platform designed to enhance safety and autonomy for individuals with limited mobility the ieee fleps international conference on flexible and printable sensors and systems is premier global forum that brings together researchers engineers and scientists to share the latest developments in flexible and printable sensors and systems and their real world applications across healthcare wearables iot and smart systems important links cccd iitj recruitment correspondence rti tenders techscape contact old website translation do you like to give feedback submit chemical engineering iit jodhpur redirecttologinpage welcome to the department of chemical engineering translation of molecular information into discovery of new products and processes with industry vision mission of the department to become globally recognized department of chemical engineering through its contribution in emerging and demand driven areas with multidisciplinary approach the department of chemical engineering offers under graduate tech and post graduate tech tech ph and ph programs in chemical engineering through the academic programs the department is making conscious effort to establish itself as leading institute in the new genre of chemical engineering education the program involves traditional courses emerging areas and specialisations such as process engineering intelligence molecular engineering and sustainability in line with the new education policy tech chemical engineering students will have unique opportunity and flexibility for minor in management entrepreneurship data science five year tech tech mba one year engineering innovation opportunity traditionally chemical engineering teaching focuses on subjects like material and energy balance transport phenomena thermodynamics mass transfer heat transfer fluid mechanics chemical reaction engineering numerical analysis computational systems biotechnology petroleum petrochemicals polymers and safety engineering with the advent of the era of industry in chemical companies artificial intelligence ai machine learning ml data analytics internet of things iot molecular engineering nano composites the discipline is being transformed by the incorporation of new emerging technologies and demand driven areas the department is keen on collaboration with industry and academia worldwide at present chemical engineering department is sharing its expertise with the department of drinking water and sanitation ddws ministry of jal shakti defence research and development organisation drdo ministry of defence international atomic energy agency iaea etc department has signed mou with the industries for providing consultancy services department is also actively involved in scientific social responsibility ssr through unnat bharat abhiyan uba and other societal initiatives department has research interest in core and emerging areas and is looking for faculty members in different areas of chemical engineering prospective candidates are encouraged to visit our recruitment page and apply highlights view all highlights congratulations to dr vikky anand assistant professor department of chemical engineering iit jodhpur for being honored as young associate of inae for the year congratulations to prof pradip kumar tewari professor department of chemical engineering iit jodhpur for honoring with the prestigious lifetime achievement award congratulations to prof parag arvind deshpande professor department
